claimText: Male Mahadeshwara (MM) Hills Wildlife Sanctuary forms a connecting corridor between the Biligiri Ranganatha Swamy Temple (BRT) Wildlife Sanctuary and the Cauvery Wildlife Sanctuary.
evidenceExcerpt: Forms connecting corridor between Biligiri Ranganatha Swamy Temple (BRT) Wildlife Sanctuary and Cauvery Wildlife Sanctuary.
examUse: Statement-based question on protected area corridors in South India.

claimText: Male Mahadeshwara (MM) Hills Wildlife Sanctuary serves as an important ecological corridor connecting the Eastern Ghats and Western Ghats landscapes.
evidenceExcerpt: Important corridor in the Eastern Ghats-Western Ghats landscape.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying sanctuaries that link the Eastern and Western Ghats.
distractorHooks: Located exclusively in the Western Ghats without Eastern Ghats linkage

claimText: The vegetation of Male Mahadeshwara (MM) Hills Wildlife Sanctuary is dominated by dry deciduous forests, with patches of semi-evergreen and Shola forests at higher altitudes.
evidenceExcerpt: Dominated by dry deciduous forests and Patches of Semi-evergreen, Shola forests at higher altitudes and scrub forests in fringe zones.
examUse: Statement-based question on the forest types found in specific wildlife sanctuaries.
distractorHooks: Dominated by tropical wet evergreen forests
Features alpine meadows at higher altitudes
